heroSection.heading: High-Performance Computing Cluster
heroSection.description: Our state-of-the-art HPC cluster provides researchers with the computational power needed for complex analyses, simulations, and data-intensive research. With 48 cores, large memory capacity, and specialized software, we enable cutting-edge research in bioinformatics, climate science, data analytics, and more.

specs.label: Memory
specs.description: Large memory capacity for data-intensive analyses

specs.label: GPU Nodes
specs.description: GPU acceleration for machine learning and simulations

hardwareDetails.description: Our HPC cluster is built with enterprise-grade hardware optimized for scientific computing and data analysis workloads.

components.specifications: 12 compute nodes, each with Intel Xeon processors (4 cores per node), 32 GB RAM, and high-speed interconnects
Dual Intel Xeon processors, 128 GB RAM, for job scheduling and cluster management
NVIDIA Tesla GPUs for accelerated computing and deep learning workloads
High-performance parallel file system with 100 TB capacity and redundancy
High-speed InfiniBand interconnect for low-latency communication between nodes

researchCapabilities.description: Our HPC cluster supports a wide range of computational research applications across multiple disciplines.

capabilities.title: Bioinformatics & Genomics
capabilities.description: Analyze large-scale genomic datasets, perform sequence alignments, variant calling, and phylogenetic analyses. Support for genome assembly, annotation, and comparative genomics studies.

capabilities.title: Machine Learning & AI
capabilities.description: Train complex machine learning models and deep neural networks for predictive analytics, image analysis, and natural language processing using GPU acceleration.

capabilities.title: Climate & Environmental Modeling
capabilities.description: Run complex climate simulations, weather forecasting models, and environmental impact assessments using parallel computing capabilities.

capabilities.title: Statistical Analysis & Data Science
capabilities.description: Perform large-scale statistical analyses, data mining, and advanced analytics on big datasets from health, agriculture, and social sciences.

softwareTools.description: Our cluster is equipped with a comprehensive suite of software tools and libraries for scientific computing and data analysis.

tools.name: GATK
tools.description: Genome Analysis Toolkit for variant discovery

tools.name: Python
tools.description: General-purpose programming with data science libraries

tools.name: OpenMPI
tools.description: Message Passing Interface for parallel computing

accessInfo.sectionTitle: How to Access the HPC Cluster
accessInfo.description: The HPC cluster is available to researchers, graduate students, and partners working on computational research projects. Access is granted based on project requirements and resource availability.
eligibility: MSc and PhD students enrolled at ACE and partner institutions
Faculty members and researchers at Makerere University
Approved external researchers with relevant projects
accessInfo.applicationProcess: To request access to the HPC cluster, submit a project proposal outlining your research objectives, computational requirements, and expected resource usage. Applications are reviewed by our technical team, and approved users receive login credentials and training on cluster usage.
accessInfo.additionalInfo: We provide technical support, user training, and consultation services to help researchers make the most of the HPC resources. Regular training sessions and workshops are conducted to build capacity in high-performance computing.

cases.title: COVID-19 Genomic Surveillance
cases.description: Researchers used the HPC cluster to analyze thousands of SARS-CoV-2 genome sequences from across Uganda, tracking viral evolution and identifying variants of concern. This work contributed to national public health responses and informed vaccination strategies.
cases.researcher: Dr. John Doe, Department of Infectious Diseases
cases.outcome: Published in leading journals and informed national COVID-19 response policy

cases.title: Climate Change Impact on Agriculture
cases.description: A team of climate scientists ran complex crop models to predict the impact of climate change on agricultural productivity in East Africa. The HPC cluster enabled them to process decades of climate data and run thousands of simulations.
cases.researcher: Prof. Jane Smith, Climate Research Unit
cases.outcome: Findings presented to policymakers and helped shape agricultural adaptation strategies

cases.title: Machine Learning for Disease Diagnosis
cases.description: Graduate students developed deep learning models to detect diseases from medical images. Using GPU acceleration on the HPC cluster, they trained models on thousands of images, achieving high diagnostic accuracy.
cases.outcome: Models are being piloted in local health facilities to improve diagnostic capacity
